notes:
Needs tweaks but after that the game works , online was not tested but local VS works as intended. Proton 3.16-7 and up recommended

Set WINEPREFIX to  /replace_with_path_to_steamapps/compatdata/388750/pfx and set this variable: WINEARCH=win32 after that use winetricks to install: devenum quartz  wmp9  directx9﻿. Create the folder on the prefix: pfx/drive_c/windows/syswow64

Compile FAudio as described in this page (make sure you have FFmpeg-dev or your distro equivalent to get WMA support)
https://github.com/FNA-XNA/FAudio/wiki/FAudio-for-Proton#part-1-building-faudio

Replace the library with the one you compiled inside the folder steamapps/common/Proton 3.16 Beta/dist/lib and lib64 respectively. Now the missing sounds work.

Disabling Esync is optional but i noticed that having it enabled may cause the game to get stuck loading screen to the main menu. The frame rate is good staying at 60 pretty much all the time but enabling anti aliasing makes the screen go black.

The game is designed for 19:10 (1280x768) so if you have a monitor with that resolution great otherwise you can select an "Arcade Mode" in the display option putting black bars on the screen or just stretching the image, keep in this causes distortion on the sprites and HUD, this is more of a problem of the game and how it was designed, not Proton.

Controllers work but they need to be plugged-in before starting the game
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: The game starts, but crashes after showing a red logo.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: The game starts, but crashes after showing a red logo.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: It was a pain to make this work. First, install protonfixes, it's easier than protontricks and you don't have to configure anything, just follow the instructions in the git page. Then install the game and protonfixes will start doing things. If the game doesn't start don't panic, the fix script takes A LOT and It will use internet conection. Then, steam won't detect that you are playing the game anymore and no window will open. Just relaunch the game and it will work. Even online works. What does not work It's antiliasing, that will make  the game black screen or crash.  So as a sumary: 1) Install protonfixes 2) Be patient with the first launch 3) Enjoy the game with no Antiliasing
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: Use protonfixes as suggested in previous reports. Also sure to also disable Esync or else you'll be stuck on infinite loading screens. After I disabled anti-aliasing, vsync and bloom in settings the game runs very smoothly.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: The game starts, but crashes after showing a red logo.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: I added protonfixes to the game in order to install it easier, but the game still plays uncomfortably. the resolution has to be 1280x768 and changing it to anything else makes the screen 'go borked' for a few seconds and then close the game. Opening the game again usually results in going back to starting resolution. The game has to be played with Esync off, or else it can get stuck loading. Changing any of the graphical settings also makes the game close, but they seem to save. They can also be manually changed using the game's config files inside the wineprefix, by removing the file on drive_c/user/steamuser/My documents/My games/bbcpex (or similar). Other than that, the game plays at full speed and it seems that all singleplayer content is playable. Haven't tested MP yet.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: The game starts, but crashes after showing a red logo.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend

notes: After installing BB CPEX, used Proton 4.2-9 with Protonfixes (as mentioned in other reports) and Esync disabled. Also installed the optional Winetricks requirement via apt-get. To run the game in a resolution other than 1280x768, disable anti-aliasing. Otherwise, everything else seems to work.
title: BlazBlue: Chronophantasma Extend
